title: Pre Workout Meal (Rice Cake Energy Stack)
metaDescription: Pre workout meal with easy-to-digest carbs and 18g of protein — rice cakes topped with turkey, banana, and honey. Ready in 5 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: pre workout meal, pre workout snack, rice cake energy stack

steps.name: Assemble
steps.text: Top each rice cake with a slice or two of turkey breast and a few banana slices.

steps.name: Drizzle
steps.text: Finish with a light drizzle of honey over each stack.

steps.name: Eat 30-60 minutes before training
steps.text: Eat within 30-60 minutes before your workout so the carbs have time to digest without feeling heavy.

tips.question: Why rice cakes instead of regular bread?
tips.answer: Rice cakes are lower in fat and fiber than most breads, which digests faster and is less likely to sit heavy in your stomach during training — that's the main reason they're a common pre-workout base.

tips.question: Why avoid high-fat or high-fiber foods before a workout?
tips.answer: Both slow digestion, which can mean the food is still sitting in your stomach when you start training — many people find this leads to sluggishness or discomfort mid-workout.

tips.question: Can I use peanut butter instead of turkey?
tips.answer: You can, but peanut butter is higher in fat, which slows digestion — if you use it, eat a bit earlier than the 30-60 minute window, or use just a thin layer.

tips.question: How much protein do I actually need before a workout?
tips.answer: Pre-workout protein needs are generally smaller than post-workout — a modest amount like the 18g here is enough for most people; the bigger priority pre-workout is usually easily digestible carbs.

storyIntro: Pre-workout nutrition gets overcomplicated fast, but the core idea is simple: carbs your body can use quickly, without a pile of fat or fiber slowing digestion down while you're trying to train.
Rice cakes topped with turkey, banana, and honey hit that easy-digest carb target with a moderate 18g of protein, and take about five minutes to put together before you walk out the door.
storyNote: Give yourself 30-60 minutes between eating this and starting your workout. Eating right before training, even something light, is the most common reason people feel sluggish or uncomfortable mid-session.
